title: Real test suite tests.py is not collected by pytest default discovery
evidence: tests.py holds 11 well-formed unittest tests that all pass when run explicitly (`pytest tests.py` -> 11 passed), but pytest's default discovery only collects test_*.py / *_test.py, so tests.py is never run by the grading harness (`pytest --cov=.`). Result: test_coverage=0.05 (scores.json) with app.py 0% covered.
suggestion: Rename tests.py to test_app.py (or add a pytest.ini with python_files=tests.py) so the real suite is discovered and the coverage/test gate reflects it.

title: test_api.py fails 0/2 under the grading harness (requires a live server)
evidence: test_api.py is a live-server smoke script that issues requests to http://localhost:5001; with no server running, pytest default discovery collects it and both tests fail with requests.exceptions.ConnectionError (Connection refused on :5001). It is the ONLY file the harness collects, so the measured suite is 0 passed / 2 failed.
suggestion: Move the live-server checks out of pytest discovery (rename to smoke_check.py or guard behind a fixture that starts the app), so headless grading isn't dominated by connection failures.

title: README documents port 5000 but the app listens on 5001
evidence: app.py:131 runs `app.run(..., port=5001)` while README.md:32 and all curl examples (README.md:50-87) use http://localhost:5000.
suggestion: Update README.md to use port 5001 (or change app.py to 5000) so the documented commands work as written.
